metadata.title: particleML Verified Literature Corpus
metadata.scope: Jet foundation models, JetClass top tagging, particle-set representations, transfer learning, evaluation, generation, and anomaly detection.

sources.title: A Method to Simultaneously Facilitate All Jet Physics Tasks
sources.verification_route: INSPIRE-HEP DOI record, Crossref metadata, and arXiv 2502.14652
sources.project_relevance: Defines the OmniLearn jet-foundation-model approach that motivates the project's pretrained PET-style backbone.
sources.central_contribution: Shows that a representation trained on a multiclass jet task can support classification, generation, likelihood-ratio estimation, anomaly detection, detector-domain changes, and collision-system changes.
sources.limitations: Its tasks, datasets, and feature interfaces are broader than the project's controlled JetClass feature-ablation protocol.

sources.title: Solving Key Challenges in Collider Physics with Foundation Models
sources.verification_route: INSPIRE-HEP arXiv record, Crossref DOI metadata, and arXiv 2404.16091
sources.project_relevance: Demonstrates downstream scientific uses of a jet foundation model beyond a proof-of-principle benchmark.
sources.central_contribution: Applies a jet foundation model to reconstruction-compute reduction, high-dimensional uncertainty quantification, and model-agnostic new-physics searches.
sources.limitations: The article evaluates application case studies rather than isolating the effect of particle-level feature availability.

sources.title: OmniJet-alpha: The First Cross-Task Foundation Model for Particle Physics
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and arXiv 2403.05618
sources.project_relevance: Provides the principal tokenized autoregressive contrast to continuous PET-style jet foundation models.
sources.central_contribution: Introduces a higher-fidelity particle tokenization and demonstrates transfer from unsupervised jet generation to supervised jet tagging.
sources.limitations: Its tokenizer and autoregressive backbone impose a different feature interface and compute profile from the project's main model.

sources.title: Foundation Model Framework for All Tasks Involving Jet Physics
sources.verification_route: INSPIRE-HEP, Crossref DOI metadata, and arXiv 2510.24066
sources.project_relevance: Supplies the pretrained OmniLearned framework and checkpoint family targeted by the current research plan.
sources.central_contribution: Upgrades OmniLearn with revised training, more than one billion training jets, public software, and demonstrations on top tagging, b-tagging, and anomaly detection.
sources.limitations: Its broad state-of-the-art claims do not by themselves establish causal effects of individual input-feature groups.

sources.title: Enhancing Next Token Prediction Based Pre-Training for Jet Foundation Models
sources.verification_route: arXiv abstract metadata
sources.project_relevance: Updates the OmniJet line with hybrid continuous inputs and combined pretraining objectives relevant to representation design.
sources.central_contribution: Reports improved downstream classification from continuous input vectors and combined masked-particle and generative pretraining without sacrificing generation quality.
sources.limitations: The work was a preprint at the search date and requires cautious use for settled performance claims.

sources.title: Aspen Open Jets: Unlocking LHC Data for Foundation Models in Particle Physics
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and arXiv 2412.10504
sources.project_relevance: Provides evidence for pretraining on experimental open data and transfer under a substantial domain shift.
sources.central_contribution: Introduces roughly 178 million CMS Open Data jets and reports that OmniJet-alpha pretraining improves generation on simulated JetClass top and QCD jets.
sources.limitations: Its downstream task is generative transfer rather than the project's supervised feature-ablation study.

sources.title: Particle Transformer for Jet Tagging
sources.venue: Proceedings of the 39th International Conference on Machine Learning, PMLR 162
sources.verification_route: Official PMLR proceedings page and arXiv 2202.03772
sources.evidence_grade: Core peer-reviewed benchmark and methods paper
sources.project_relevance: Introduces both the JetClass benchmark and the particle-interaction-aware transformer architecture used as a reference for PET-style modeling.
sources.central_contribution: Presents a 100-million-jet, ten-class dataset and a transformer that incorporates pairwise particle interactions and benefits from pretraining.
sources.limitations: The headline study is multiclass tagging; the project must define and freeze its own binary top-versus-QCD extraction.

sources.title: JetClass: A Large-Scale Dataset for Deep Learning in Jet Physics
sources.verification_route: Official Zenodo record and DOI metadata
sources.project_relevance: Defines the provenance and public file release for the project's primary dataset.
sources.central_contribution: Provides the large simulated jet corpus used for JetClass benchmarking and the project's top-versus-QCD study.
sources.limitations: A repository record does not specify the project's exact file subset, split manifest, feature transformations, or leakage controls.

sources.title: Maximizing Boosted Top Identification by Minimizing N-Subjettiness
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and journal record
sources.project_relevance: Establishes the classical boosted-top-tagging context against which learned particle-level representations are motivated.
sources.central_contribution: Develops N-subjettiness observables and demonstrates their use for discriminating boosted hadronic top jets.
sources.limitations: It predates modern particle-cloud networks and is a physics-observable baseline rather than a foundation-model study.

sources.title: The Machine Learning Landscape of Top Taggers
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and arXiv 1902.09914
sources.project_relevance: Provides a shared boosted-top-tagging comparison landscape and cautions against overinterpreting architecture-only differences.
sources.central_contribution: Compares a wide range of low-level-input top taggers and finds broadly similar performance across substantially different architectures.
sources.limitations: The benchmark predates current jet foundation models and does not test pretrained feature-ablation protocols.

sources.title: JetNet: A Python Package for Accessing Open Datasets and Benchmarking Machine Learning Methods in High Energy Physics
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and JOSS record
sources.project_relevance: Documents a reproducible interface for open jet datasets and common generation and tagging benchmarks.
sources.central_contribution: Packages jet datasets, evaluation metrics, and reference workflows to improve benchmarking consistency in HEP machine learning.
sources.limitations: Its packaged datasets and defaults do not replace the project's explicit JetClass provenance and split manifest.

sources.title: Deep Sets
sources.venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
sources.verification_route: Official NeurIPS proceedings page and arXiv 1703.06114
sources.project_relevance: Provides the mathematical basis for a simple permutation-invariant baseline over jet constituents.
sources.central_contribution: Characterizes permutation-invariant set functions and gives a practical sum-decomposition neural architecture.
sources.limitations: The basic aggregation form does not explicitly model pairwise particle interactions or Lorentz structure.

sources.title: Set Transformer: A Framework for Attention-Based Permutation-Invariant Neural Networks
sources.venue: Proceedings of the 36th International Conference on Machine Learning, PMLR 97
sources.verification_route: Official PMLR proceedings page and arXiv 1810.00825
sources.project_relevance: Supplies a general attention-based set architecture relevant to unordered variable-length particle inputs.
sources.central_contribution: Introduces attention modules for permutation-invariant learning and an inducing-point mechanism that reduces self-attention cost.
sources.limitations: It is a domain-general set model and does not encode jet-specific pairwise physics or detector features.

sources.title: Jet Tagging via Particle Clouds
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and arXiv 1902.08570
sources.project_relevance: Establishes the particle-cloud representation and a strong graph-based benchmark for jet tagging.
sources.central_contribution: Adapts dynamic graph convolution to unordered particle clouds and reports strong performance on representative tagging benchmarks.
sources.limitations: ParticleNet is a supervised task-specific architecture, not a pretrained cross-task foundation model.

sources.title: Point Cloud Transformers Applied to Collider Physics
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and arXiv 2102.05073
sources.project_relevance: Provides the PET precursor that directly informs the OmniLearn and OmniLearned backbone family.
sources.central_contribution: Adapts transformer-based relation learning to unordered collider point clouds and evaluates it on boosted-particle jet tagging.
sources.limitations: The study focuses on supervised tagging and predates the full multi-task foundation-model training regime.

sources.title: An Efficient Lorentz Equivariant Graph Neural Network for Jet Tagging
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and arXiv 2201.08187
sources.project_relevance: Provides a high-performing comparison for how explicit Lorentz symmetry can alter data efficiency and generalization.
sources.central_contribution: Introduces LorentzNet with Minkowski-dot-product message passing and reports competitive tagging with improved efficiency at small training sizes.
sources.limitations: It tests a different inductive bias and architecture, so performance cannot be directly attributed to the project's feature ladder.

sources.title: Energy Flow Networks: Deep Sets for Particle Jets
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and arXiv 1810.05165
sources.project_relevance: Directly distinguishes IRC-safe energy-flow inputs from particle-flow models that admit charge, flavor, and other particle features.
sources.central_contribution: Specializes Deep Sets to jets, introduces EFNs and PFNs, and shows how per-particle feature choices determine the allowed information content.
sources.limitations: The quark-versus-gluon demonstrations do not isolate the same feature groups or use a pretrained foundation-model backbone.

sources.title: Energy Flow Polynomials: A Complete Linear Basis for Jet Substructure
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and arXiv 1712.07124
sources.project_relevance: Provides an interpretable basis for discussing which physically meaningful information is available to learned low-level representations.
sources.central_contribution: Constructs a complete linear basis for infrared- and collinear-safe jet observables and demonstrates strong linear tagging performance.
sources.limitations: The basis excludes non-IRC-safe information such as particle identity and charge that the project's richer feature views may exploit.

sources.title: Symmetries, Safety, and Self-Supervision
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and arXiv 2108.04253
sources.project_relevance: Connects permutation invariance, physical symmetries, and self-supervised jet representations to label-efficient downstream tagging.
sources.central_contribution: Introduces JetCLR, a contrastive permutation-invariant transformer representation, and evaluates it with linear probes on top and QCD jets.
sources.limitations: Its contrastive pretraining objective and linear-probe evaluation differ from end-to-end fine-tuning of OmniLearned.

sources.title: A Survey on Transfer Learning
sources.venue: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and IEEE record
sources.project_relevance: Supplies standard terminology for source tasks, target tasks, domains, and transfer assumptions.
sources.central_contribution: Organizes transfer learning by problem setting and method family and formalizes when knowledge is moved between domains or tasks.
sources.limitations: The survey predates modern foundation models and does not address HEP-specific simulation or feature-schema constraints.

sources.title: How Transferable Are Features in Deep Neural Networks?
sources.venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
sources.verification_route: arXiv metadata and NeurIPS 2014 journal reference
sources.project_relevance: Provides a conceptual basis for separating transferable backbone representations from task-specific input adapters and upper layers.
sources.central_contribution: Empirically distinguishes layer specialization from optimization difficulties when features are transferred and fine-tuned across tasks.
sources.limitations: The experiments use image networks, so the conclusions motivate rather than validate jet-specific adapter choices.

sources.title: On the Opportunities and Risks of Foundation Models
sources.verification_route: arXiv abstract metadata and Stanford CRFM report record
sources.project_relevance: Provides the broad definition of models trained at scale and adapted to multiple downstream tasks used in the paper's framing.
sources.central_contribution: Defines the foundation-model paradigm and surveys its technical principles, cross-domain opportunities, inherited risks, and evaluation challenges.
sources.limitations: It is a broad cross-domain report and cannot support HEP-specific performance or reproducibility claims.

sources.title: Machine Learning at the Energy and Intensity Frontiers of Particle Physics
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and Nature record
sources.project_relevance: Places jet machine learning and transferable learned representations within the wider HEP analysis ecosystem.
sources.central_contribution: Reviews machine-learning applications, opportunities, and deployment constraints across particle-physics experiments.
sources.limitations: The review predates jet foundation models and therefore provides context rather than direct evidence for the planned comparison.

sources.title: Evaluation Metrics and Statistical Tests for Machine Learning
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and publisher record
sources.project_relevance: Supports explicit metric selection and the distinction between descriptive performance and inferential comparison.
sources.central_contribution: Reviews common ML evaluation metrics and statistical tests and discusses when each is appropriate.
sources.limitations: Its examples are domain-general and do not prescribe HEP-specific operating points or systematic uncertainties.

sources.title: Comparing the Areas Under Two or More Correlated Receiver Operating Characteristic Curves: A Nonparametric Approach
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and journal record
sources.project_relevance: Provides a standard nonparametric method for comparing ROC AUCs evaluated on the same examples.
sources.central_contribution: Derives covariance-aware comparisons for two or more correlated ROC curves.
sources.limitations: Seed-to-seed training variability and nested data subsampling require additional hierarchical or resampling treatment.

sources.title: Approximate Statistical Tests for Comparing Supervised Classification Learning Algorithms
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and journal record
sources.project_relevance: Warns that naïve reuse of train/test splits can invalidate significance tests when comparing learning algorithms.
sources.central_contribution: Analyzes approximate tests for classifier comparisons and documents inflated error rates from common resampling procedures.
sources.limitations: The recommended procedures were developed before modern deep-learning seed and compute variance became central concerns.

sources.title: Inference for the Generalization Error
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and Springer record
sources.project_relevance: Supports corrected uncertainty reasoning when performance estimates share overlapping training data.
sources.central_contribution: Studies variance estimation for generalization error and proposes corrections for dependence induced by repeated data reuse.
sources.limitations: Its analytical assumptions may not fully capture stochastic deep-network optimization or the project's nested feature views.

sources.title: Improving Reproducibility in Machine Learning Research: A Report from the NeurIPS 2019 Reproducibility Program
sources.verification_route: Official JMLR article page
sources.project_relevance: Supports artifact, checklist, code, and reporting practices needed for a credible multi-seed computational study.
sources.central_contribution: Reports the design and outcomes of a large conference reproducibility program and identifies practical reporting interventions.
sources.limitations: It provides process evidence rather than a domain-specific recipe for JetClass training and uncertainty analysis.

sources.title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
sources.venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
sources.verification_route: arXiv metadata and NeurIPS 2020 proceedings record
sources.project_relevance: Provides the diffusion-model basis for modern particle-cloud generators used as future-work context.
sources.central_contribution: Develops a practical denoising diffusion objective and demonstrates high-quality generative modeling through iterative noise reversal.
sources.limitations: The experiments use image data and do not address variable-length particle sets or HEP validation metrics.

sources.title: PC-JeDi: Diffusion for Particle Cloud Generation in High Energy Physics
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and arXiv 2303.05376
sources.project_relevance: Demonstrates diffusion-based conditional generation directly on permutation-equivariant particle clouds.
sources.central_contribution: Introduces a transformer score model for top and gluon jet generation and evaluates it with multiple fidelity metrics.
sources.limitations: Generation quality and sampling speed are distinct outcomes from supervised tagging AUC and feature ablation.

sources.title: EPiC-GAN: Equivariant Point Cloud Generation for Particle Jets
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and arXiv 2301.08128
sources.project_relevance: Provides an efficient variable-multiplicity point-cloud generator aligned with the project's set-based representation context.
sources.central_contribution: Uses equivariant deep-set layers and a global latent vector to generate particle jets without expensive pairwise message passing.
sources.limitations: It studies adversarial generation rather than transfer learning or controlled changes in input features.

sources.title: LHC Hadronic Jet Generation Using Convolutional Variational Autoencoders with Normalizing Flows
sources.verification_route: INSPIRE-HEP, Crossref DOI metadata, and arXiv 2310.13138
sources.project_relevance: Supplies a contrasting latent-variable generation pipeline and examples of jet-generation validation.
sources.central_contribution: Combines convolutional variational autoencoders with normalizing flows to generate LHC hadronic jets and benchmark fidelity.
sources.limitations: Its image-like convolutional representation is not directly comparable to OmniLearned particle-set fine-tuning.

sources.title: Anomaly Detection for Resonant New Physics with Machine Learning
sources.verification_route: INSPIRE-HEP, Crossref DOI metadata, and arXiv 1805.02664
sources.project_relevance: Provides a canonical model-agnostic collider-anomaly use case for transferable low-level representations.
sources.central_contribution: Introduces a weakly supervised resonant anomaly search that uses sidebands and auxiliary features to enhance a bump hunt.
sources.limitations: Its signal-localization assumptions and search statistic differ from generic representation-level anomaly detection.

sources.title: The LHC Olympics 2020: A Community Challenge for Anomaly Detection in High Energy Physics
sources.verification_route: Crossref DOI metadata and arXiv 2101.08320
sources.project_relevance: Defines standard datasets, evaluation lessons, and practical failure modes for model-agnostic collider anomaly detection.
sources.central_contribution: Synthesizes a large community challenge using hidden-signal black boxes and compares diverse unsupervised and weakly supervised methods.
sources.limitations: The challenge focuses on anomaly-search performance and does not test the project's feature-ablation hypothesis.
